commands.review_summary:
The PR passes Discord inbound `threadId` into the Discord conversation resolver, returns raw thread IDs for inbound thread conversations, and adds a focused helper regression test for thread versus top-level identities.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +27. Total +34 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current main passes `threadId` to channel resolvers, Discord ignores it, and plugin-owned binding lookup later keys off the returned conversation id. I did not run a live Discord reproduction in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(discord): route thread bindings to plugin owners
target.body:
## Summary

- Route Discord inbound thread conversations by their raw thread id before falling back to the parent channel identity.
- Pass Discord inbound `threadId` through the channel plugin messaging resolver so plugin-owned bindings can claim thread turns.
- Add a focused regression test proving thread conversations use the raw thread id while top-level channels keep the `channel:<id>` identity.

Fixes #64199.
Related: #87721, #84086, #64322.

## Behavior

Discord `/codex bind --cwd ...` follow-up messages in a thread could fall back to the parent OpenClaw agent/session instead of the bound Codex session. The visible symptom was that asking the bound session who it was still answered as the OpenClaw agent, and cwd stayed on the agent workspace instead of the cwd selected during bind.

The Discord plugin already owns the conversation identity rules for this route. This change keeps top-level channel identities prefixed, but lets inbound thread messages resolve to the raw Discord thread id so the plugin-owned binding can match the actual bound Codex session.

## Verification

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/channel.conversation.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Discord `/codex bind --cwd ...` follow-up routing to the bound Codex session instead of the parent OpenClaw agent session.

Real environment tested: User-confirmed Discord flow on the original fix branch `fix/discord-plugin-owned-binding-route`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: In Discord, bind a Codex session with `/codex bind --cwd <workspace>`, then send follow-up messages asking the session who it is and which cwd it is using.

Evidence after fix: The requester confirmed the branch fixes the Discord Codex native session binding route.

Observed result after fix: The bound Discord session responds as Codex and uses the cwd selected during bind instead of the parent OpenClaw agent workspace.

What was not tested: I did not rerun a live Discord end-to-end flow from this clean PR branch; I verified the focused unit regression locally and preserved the user-confirmed patch as a clean cherry-pick.
